rejected_candidates.title: Flash loan returns funds without verification, enabling fund theft
rejected_candidates.reason: Incorrect. The post-loan check at `1607-1650` requires `_fundSource` or the pool to end with exactly the same balance as before when `amountFee == 0`, so the borrower cannot keep the funds.

rejected_candidates.title: Block-based loan limit can be bypassed within same block
rejected_candidates.reason: Incorrect. `updateBlockLoanAmount()` adds the new amount first and immediately reverts if the cumulative amount for the current block reaches or exceeds `blockLoanLimit`, so bundling transactions does not bypass the cap.

rejected_candidates.title: Signature can be replayed across different loans with same parameters
rejected_candidates.reason: Not independently reportable here. A single NFT cannot hold concurrent loans, and reusing the same valuation for the same NFT before expiry is largely the intended behavior of this verifier.

rejected_candidates.title: Repay can result in underflow when repayAmount less than outstanding
rejected_candidates.reason: The claimed arithmetic-underflow path is not the real issue. The actual repayment failure is the outbound `transferFrom(address(this), ...)` bug captured separately.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing check for loan existence before liquidation
rejected_candidates.reason: The specific claim about liquidating already repaid NFTs is incorrect because full repayment transfers the NFT out before loan terms are cleared. The broader liquidation-trust issue is already captured separately.

rejected_candidates.title: No validation that _controlPlane is a valid contract
rejected_candidates.reason: This is owner/configuration hygiene rather than an intrinsic exploit path in the pool.

rejected_candidates.title: Precision loss in fee calculations
rejected_candidates.reason: This is ordinary integer truncation with negligible impact and is not reportable.

rejected_candidates.title: PineWallet clone validation only checks factory whitelist
rejected_candidates.reason: Insufficient support from the available code. The pool also requires ownership of the derived mirror token, and any exploit would depend on external factory or wallet behavior not shown here.

rejected_candidates.title: Valuation signatures are replayable across pools and borrowing contexts
rejected_candidates.reason: Too speculative as a standalone issue. The signature appears to authorize valuation data only, while amount and duration are intentionally bounded by pool parameters; concrete cross-pool harm would require extra deployment or signer-reuse assumptions.
